---
title: "Tutorial: public survival data"
output: rmarkdown::html_vignette
vignette: >
  %\VignetteIndexEntry{Tutorial: public survival data}
  %\VignetteEngine{knitr::rmarkdown}
  %\VignetteEncoding{UTF-8}
---

```{r, include = FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(collapse = TRUE, comment = "#>")
if (requireNamespace("pkgload", quietly = TRUE)) {
  if (file.exists("DESCRIPTION")) {
    pkgload::load_all(".", export_all = FALSE, helpers = FALSE, attach_testthat = FALSE)
  } else if (file.exists("../DESCRIPTION")) {
    pkgload::load_all("..", export_all = FALSE, helpers = FALSE, attach_testthat = FALSE)
  } else {
    library(cce)
  }
} else {
  library(cce)
}
library(survival)
```

## Use `survival::veteran`

`survival::veteran` is a public dataset already distributed with the
`survival` package, so this tutorial needs no external download.

```{r}
data(veteran, package = "survival")

veteran2 <- transform(
  veteran,
  arm = ifelse(trt == 1, "SOC", "A"),
  time = time,
  event = status,
  subgroup = as.character(celltype)
)

head(veteran2[, c("arm", "time", "event", "subgroup")])
```

## Run the observed comparison

```{r}
vs_fit <- fit_cce_vs(
  data = veteran2,
  subgroup = "subgroup",
  tau = 180,
  times = c(90, 180)
)

head(as_effects_df(vs_fit))
```

## Run the SOC-only projection

```{r}
soc_fit <- project_soc_only(
  data = veteran2,
  subgroup = "subgroup",
  tau = 180,
  hr_scenarios = c(0.75, 0.90, 1.00),
  target_delta_rmst = 10
)

head(as_effects_df(soc_fit))
```
